steps.title: Rewrite each term as a square
steps.content:
Both terms are perfect squares. The coefficient $324$ is $18^2$, and the constant $900$ is $30^2$.
$$324w^2=(18w)^2$$
$$900=30^2$$
So the expression can be written as a difference of squares.
$$(18w)^2-30^2$$

steps.title: Apply the square difference pattern
steps.content:
A difference of squares factors into the difference of the bases times the sum of the bases.
$$a^2-b^2=(a-b)(a+b)$$
Here, $a=18w$ and $b=30$, so the expression factors as
$$(18w-30)(18w+30)$$

steps.title: Match the factored expression
steps.content: The original expression has a subtraction between two squares, so the factored form needs one factor with subtraction and one factor with addition. The expression $(18w-30)(18w+30)$ matches that structure and expands back to $324w^2-900$. The answer is D.
